3GPP_Reference.Title: NG-RAN; F1 Application Protocol (F1AP), NG-RAN; Architecture description

Feature_Description: The L1/L2 Triggered Mobility (LTM) handover procedure enables a UE to move within the same gNB-DU (Intra-gNB-DU LTM) or from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU (Inter-gNB-DU LTM) during NR operation.

The UE Context Modification procedure, initiated by the gNB-CU, is used to modify the established UE Context, including establishing, modifying, and releasing radio resources or sidelink resources. For LTM, this procedure is specifically used to configure LTM-specific information, handle early synchronization, and manage cell switches. The gNB-CU sends a 'UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST' message to the gNB-DU, which then performs the requested modifications and responds with a 'UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE' message.
Feature_Objectives: To enable L1/L2 Triggered Mobility (LTM) for a UE within the same gNB-DU or between different gNB-DUs under the same gNB-CU.
To configure LTM-specific information, such as CSI resource configuration and LTM configuration ID mapping.
To facilitate early synchronization (early TA acquisition) for LTM candidate cells.
To manage the release of LTM cells and associated resources.
To provide lower layer configuration for generating LTM reference configurations.
Feature_Implementation_Steps: 1. The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration based on L3 measurement reports from the UE.

2. The gNB-CU sends a 'UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST' message to the gNB-DU for each candidate cell. This message contains a candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may also provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list, request PRACH resources, request the gNB-DU to provide the lower layer configuration for generating the reference configuration, or provide the lower layer reference configuration to the gNB-DU. It may also inform the gNB-DU about intra-DU L2 reset configuration.

3. If the gNB-DU accepts the LTM configuration request, it responds with a 'UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE' message, including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted candidate cell. This response may also include the LTM Configuration IE, Early Sync Information IE, and Requested Target Cell ID IE.

4. The gNB-CU may send further 'UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST' messages to the gNB-DU to update the LTM configuration ID mapping list, CSI resource configuration, or intra-DU L2 reset configuration.
5. The gNB-DU responds with a 'UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE' message, which may include an updated lower layer configuration.
6. The gNB-CU sends a 'DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER' message to the gNB-DU, which includes the generated RRCReconfiguration message with the LTM configuration.
7. The gNB-DU forwards the received RRCReconfiguration message to the UE.
8. The UE responds to the gNB-DU with an RRCReconfigurationComplete message.
9. The gNB-DU forwards the RRCReconfigurationComplete message to the gNB-CU via an 'UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER' message.
10. The gNB-CU sends a 'CU-DU MOBILITY INITIATION REQUEST' message to the gNB-DU to trigger early synchronization and/or cell switch.
11. Early TA acquisition is performed.
12. The UE sends L1 measurement reports to the gNB-DU.
13. The gNB-DU makes an LTM Cell Switch Decision.
14. The gNB-DU sends a Cell Switch Command to the UE.
15. The gNB-DU sends a 'DU-CU CELL SWITCH NOTIFICATION' message to the gNB-CU, indicating the target cell ID.
16. The gNB-DU detects the UE access.
17. The gNB-DU sends an 'ACCESS SUCCESS' message to the gNB-CU, indicating the target cell ID.
18. The UE sends an RRCReconfigurationComplete message to the gNB-DU.
19. The gNB-DU forwards the RRCReconfigurationComplete message to the gNB-CU via an 'UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER' message.
20. The gNB-CU sends a 'UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST' message to the gNB-DU to release the prepared cells.
21. The gNB-DU responds with a 'UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION RESPONSE' message.

Call_Flow.Message: UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST (Prepared cells to be released)

LTMInformation-Modify ::= SEQUENCE {
 lTMIndicator      LTMIndicator,
 referenceConfiguration    ReferenceConfiguration         OPTIONAL,
 cSIResourceConfiguration    CSIResourceConfiguration        OPTIONAL,
 iE-Extensions  ProtocolExtensionContainer { { LTMInformation-Modify-ExtIEs} } OPTIONAL,
 ...
}

LTMConfiguration ::= SEQUENCE {
 sSBInformation     SSBInformation,
 referenceConfigurationInformation  ReferenceConfigurationInformation   OPTIONAL,
 completeCandidateConfigurationIndicator  CompleteCandidateConfigurationIndicator     OPTIONAL,
 lTMCFRAResourceConfig    LTMCFRAResourceConfig      OPTIONAL,
 lTMCFRAResourceConfigSUL   LTMCFRAResourceConfig      OPTIONAL,
 iE-Extensions      ProtocolExtensionContainer { { LTMConfiguration-ExtIEs } } OPTIONAL,
 ...
}

Information_Elements.IE_Name: Early UL Sync Configuration for SUL IE

Piggy_Backed_IES_Info: The RRCReconfiguration message, which includes the LTM configuration, is piggy-backed within the F1AP DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message. Similarly, the RRCReconfigurationComplete message is piggy-backed within the F1AP UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message.

8.2.1.3 (ts_138401v180600p): Inter-gNB-DU Conditional Handover or Conditional PSCell Change or subsequent CPAC

8.19.4.2 (ts_138401v180600p): Intra-gNB-DU switch from direct to indirect path
8.2.2.3 (ts_138401v180600p): Inter-gNB-DU Conditional PSCell Change using MCG SRB without MN negotiation
8.3.1 (ts_138401v180600p): Centralized Retransmission in Intra gNB-CU Cases
8.14.1.3 (ts_138401v180600p): Reporting of DL LBT failure information from gNB-DU to gNB-CU
8.19.4.1 (ts_138401v180600p): Inter-gNB-DU switch from direct to indirect path
8.14.1.2 (ts_138401v180600p): Signalling of RACH information from gNB-CU to gNB-DU

9.4.2 (ts_138473v180600p): Usage of private message mechanism for non-standard use
